ecosystemflora:overview-text: Ecosystem - Flora Wild plants spread, compete, follow seasons, and can die from niche stress. Every block stays vanilla — only behaviour changes. Spread and competition Mature plants try to occupy nearby cells. A stronger species can displace a weaker one. Plants in the wrong place accumulate stress and die; ferns and many berry bushes need a host tree nearby. Ecology inspect — I Aim at a wild plant, tree trunk, mushroom, or mycelium soil for a live report: registry status, stress, next spread, season, mat edge (reeds/lilies), tree age and size, mycelium network. Details on the Ecology Inspect page. Trees Mature trunks scatter saplings; optional yearly aging and death at species lifespan. Calendar age is saved with the world. See Trees and Seasonal Canopy. Scope Ecology runs on plants in loaded chunks. Wild spread, aging, and senescence do not run inside land claims. Simulation (v3.8) Spread is scheduled chunk-fair across loaded areas. Nearby block changes wake neighbors for a quick retry; each in-game month wakes seasonal species. Break turf or fell a tree — the meadow reacts within a couple of spread ticks. Registration When chunks load, flora registers incrementally. Near you, priority queues and burst scans finish quickly — inspect (I) sees meadow plants while you explore. Config ModConfig/ecosystemflora.json — presets natural, lush, sparse. See Configuration Guide.
ecosystemflora:flowers-text: Meadow Flowers (21 species) Flowers shape meadow succession: quick colonizers appear first, slower species hold ground once rooted. Each has climate, soil, light, and seasonal spread — per-flower values appear on the block handbook page and in ecology inspect (I). Fast colonizers Horsetail, heather, western gorse, red top grass, mugwort, cowparsley, lupine. Spread fast, hold weakly; later species often displace them. Heather and gorse slightly dry soil while living; lupine slowly enriches soil. Steady meadow Catmint, cornflower, wild daisy, forget-me-not, woad. Moderate spread and hold; open ground to partial shade. Slow / localized Daffodil, ghost pipes (white, pink, red), orange mallow, edelweiss, golden poppy. Strong hold, narrow climates — rare outside their niche. Forest Bluebell, lily of the valley, ghost pipes. Need canopy shade and symbiosis with nearby trees. Harvest Empty hand — flower block. Knife or scythe — dry grass (scythe mows all meadow flowers).
ecosystemflora:ferns-text: Ferns (5 species) Forest edge and understory. Prefer shade and moisture; need a host tree within a few blocks (symbiosis). Fell the tree and ferns build stress until they die. Strongest on forest borders — Edge context bonus. Species Eagle fern — fast edge colonizer. Cinnamon and deer fern — moderate. Hart's tongue — deep shade, slow. Tall fern — two blocks high. Minimum sunlight 7 under canopy. Spread rate, climate, and niche on each block's handbook page.
ecosystemflora:trees-text: Trees (14 species) Sapling spread A mature log-grown trunk can place a free sapling on open soil nearby when light is sufficient. From sapling to adult, vanilla treegen handles growth — the mod only sets the sapling. Between November and February trees do not spread. Press I on any log: climate and soil are taken from the trunk base, not from mid-air blocks. Aging (EnableTreeAging, on by default) Each game year in loaded chunks, registered trunks gain one calendar year and may add a log, branchy block, or leaves unless already full-sized. Age starts at 0 when the tree enters ecology — a tall worldgen tree can be age 0. Age is stored in the save and restored when the chunk rescans the trunk after a restart. Senescence (EnableTreeSenescence, on by default) Each species has a lifespan (oak about 120 years, birch less). After that, death unfolds over four game years: crown leaves fall and spread stops; branchy skeleton is cleared; a short dry snag stands; then the snag collapses to a **choppable stump** and a few **fallen logs** on the ground (`EnableTreeSenescenceRemains`, `TreeSenescenceFallenLogCount`). Set remains off to clear to air instead. Inside a land claim, steps are blocked. Species: oak, pine, birch, maple, acacia, kapok, walnut, larch, redwood, ebony, purpleheart, baldcypress, mangrove, joshua. Bamboo and aged wood are not included. Trunks do not die from stress — only from senescence when enabled.
ecosystemflora:berries-text: Berry Bushes (10 wild species) Slow spread, strong hold. Most species prefer forest edge (Edge context), forest-floor or medium-fertility soil, and nearby trees for best fitness. Species Blueberry, blackberry, black currant, red currant, white currant, cranberry, beautyberry, cloudberry, lingonberry. Strawberry Open meadow — faster spread, weaker hold. Soil With UseSoilSuccession enabled, bushes enrich soil during life and on natural death.
ecosystemflora:aquatic-text: Aquatic Flora (5 species) Placement depends on substrate and water depth. Three spread patterns. Rhizome mat — cattail, tule, papyrus: only stems on the stand edge advance one block at a time. Rare seeds or fragments (~6–10% of attempts) can land farther along the bank. Floating mat — water lily: edge pads fill adjacent open water (including diagonals). Rare seeds (~5%) across open water. Radius — water crowfoot: underwater column on gravel. Ecology inspect (I) shows mat edge and seed chance. Cattail Shore on gravel or muddy gravel with water within 3 blocks, or exactly 1 water block over a gravel bed. No spread into deep water (2+ blocks). One stem per column. Harvest: stems then roots (vanilla). Tule Same placement as cattail; temperate climate (5–25°C); slightly slower rhizome spread. Папirus Warm climate (24–40°C); 2 blocks tall; 1 water layer over substrate. Water lily Floating mat on still open water. Water crowfoot Underwater column, 2–8 blocks deep on gravel or muddy gravel; ecology tracks the base block; flowering top on ~35% of plants.
ecosystemflora:canopy-text: Seasonal canopy Deciduous log-grown trees — oak, birch, maple, and others — change foliage by season. Conifers are unchanged. Autumn — branchy crown thins unevenly; colour shows through the skeleton. Winter — bare branchy frame can remain. Spring — buds on bark, branchy refills, normal leaves return. Runs in loaded chunks; state catches up when you visit. Optional leaf particles near tall crowns (EnableCanopyAmbience). Seasonal foliage and tree aging are separate: this page is crown dress by season; calendar growth and senescence are on Trees. Disable with EnableSeasonalFoliage for static vanilla crowns.
ecosystemflora:inspect-text: Ecology inspect Hotkey: I (configurable). Server-side report for the block you aim at. Valid targets Meadow flowers and grass, ferns, berries, aquatic plants, any log-grown on a wild tree (one report per tree), mushroom caps and mycelium on forest floor, soil, or peat. Typical lines Registry status, stress, next spread attempt, seasonal multiplier, niche fit, symbiosis, climate survival. Reeds/lilies: spread mode, mat edge, seed chance. Trees: calendar age vs lifespan, trunk/crown size %. Mycelium: network edge, anchor stress. Trees Age counts from ecology registration, not worldgen. Large worldgen trees can show full size at age 0. Soil and climate always from the trunk base. Disabled inside land claims. Cooldown and scan radius: EnableEcologyInspect and related keys in config.
ecosystemflora:tuning-text: Configuration Guide File: ModConfig/ecosystemflora.json. Missing keys are filled in when the mod loads. Presets natural (default) — ~72 spread attempts/year, ~50% success. lush — faster meadows, 120 attempts. sparse — subtle, 36 attempts. custom — manual values only. Main toggles EcosystemEnabled, seasonal ecology, displacement, stress death, symbiosis, land claims, trampling (off), ecology inspect (I). Trees EnableTreeAging, EnableTreeSenescence, TreeSenescenceSnagBlocks (default 3), EnableTreeSenescenceRemains, TreeSenescenceFallenLogCount. Calendar age and senescence phase persist in the save. Canopy & mycelium EnableSeasonalFoliage, EnableCanopyAmbience, EnableMyceliumEcology, network spread. Soil UseSoilSuccession, EnableFallowRestoration. Performance OnlyActivateNearPlayers defaults to false — ecology on all plants in loaded chunks. Set true only to limit activity to ~192 blocks from players (testing). Simulation engine (v3.8) Recommended defaults (no geographic cutoffs): EnableChunkFairSpread, EnableEventDrivenSpread, EnableEcologyColumnCache, EnableTwoPhaseSpreadPlacement, EnableSeasonCoarseWake — all true. Spread runs round-robin per loaded chunk; break/place/succession wakes neighbors; each in-game month wakes seasonal species. Tune fairness with MaxSpreadAttemptsPerChunkPerTick and MaxSpreadChunksVisitedPerTick. Legacy safety only: LimitSpreadNearPlayers (spread + stress + tree aging near players; chunk registration unchanged), OnlyActivateNearPlayers (playtest — also limits chunk scans), TickBudgetMs 30, SpreadBudgetMs 30. Registration (v3.8) EnablePlayerPriorityRegistration, EnableBurstRegistrationNearPlayers, PlayerRegistrationPriorityRadiusBlocks (default 16), BurstRegistrationBudgetMs 80. EnableBackgroundRegistrationScan — block ids on main, flower/vine/tree classify on worker; canopy foliage stays on main. Throughput: MaxRegistrationSnapshotCellsPerTick 8192, MaxRegistryAppliesPerTick 512, MaxPriorityRegistryAppliesPerTick 2048. Vines register via column pass; mycelium anchors via chunk BE scan. Tick intervals: ReproduceTickIntervalMs 2000, ChunkScanTickIntervalMs 2300, StressTickIntervalMs 5500. Spread collect (v3.8) EnableEmptyFirstSpreadCollect — scan empty/vacancy cells first with full fitness. EnableSpreadColumnOccupancyHint — skip columns already holding ecology plants. Displacement still runs when no empty cell qualifies.
ecosystemflora:handbook-season-spread: Seasonal spread: Spring {0}x, Summer {1}x, Fall {2}x, Winter {3}x
ecosystemflora:dominance-climax: Climax (slow to spread, hard to displace)
ecosystemflora:dominance-grass-colonizer: Grass colonizer (invades turf, competes with tallgrass)
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-notarget: Aim at a wild plant to inspect.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-noplant: That block is not part of the ecosystem.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-disabled: Ecology inspect is disabled in config.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-claim: Cannot inspect inside a land claim.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-cooldown: Wait a moment before inspecting again.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-dialog: Could not open ecology inspect window.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-not-register: Not in spread registry (vanilla block only).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-tree-senesce: Senescent — past natural lifespan; phased decline begins on the next ecology year tick.
Senescence — crown leaves falling; sapling spread stopped.
Senescence — bare branchy skeleton; trunk still standing.
Senescence — dry standing trunk (snag); stump + fallen logs next year.
Approaching senescence — natural death next game year if aging is active.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-ferntree-sen: Senescence — short trunk only; collapse next year.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-tree-size: Structure: trunk {0} blocks, crown radius {1} ({2}% of typical mature ~{3}/{4}).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-symbiosis-mi: Symbiosis: no host tree — at risk.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-no-climate: No climate data at this position.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mat-frontier: Mat edge: yes — can spread outward.
Mat edge: no — interior; only rare seed may jump.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-anc: Mycelium anchor underfoot — meadow spread blocked.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-mea: Near mycelium zone ({0} blocks): meadow spread ×{1}.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-for: Near mycelium zone ({0} blocks): forest spread ×{1}.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-unr: Mycelium BE present but not registered yet.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-no-: No mycelium block entity under this cap — ecology data from mushroom type only.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-sur: Anchor niche: stressed (wrong forest / no tree host).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-fro: Network edge: yes — can spread outward.
Network edge: no — interior of mat.
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-no-: Climate survival: no solid ground below.
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-soi: Climate survival: soil type mismatch (here: {0}; needs one of: {1}).
Climate survival: soil not fertile enough ({0} < {1}).
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-for: Climate survival: local forest cover too sparse ({0} < {1}).
Climate survival: local forest cover too dense ({0} > {1}).
